mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate earnings and thus positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in more than 40% of periods where RSAFS month-over-month growth is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) below a critical threshold signals a tightening labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative or flat (within +/- 0.5%) in more than 50% of periods when UNRATE is below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is at historically low levels and stable, it indicates an accommodative monetary environment that encourages investment and supports equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in more than 35% of periods where FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with strong industrial production (INDPRO) suggests an overheating economy, which could prompt tighter monetary policy and erode real earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change > 3.0% AND INDPRO 3-month annualized change > 5.0%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the Rank IC is consistently positive.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent high levels of housing starts (HOUST) reflect strong consumer and business confidence, indicating a healthy economic environment that supports overall market growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in more than 45% of periods where HOUST is above its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration in non-farm payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth, even if still positive, signals a weakening labor market and potential economic slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth rate falls below 0.1% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 50% of periods where PAYEMS growth decelerates below 0.1%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating GDP growth (GDPC1) during a recovery phase indicates a strengthening economy, which typically boosts corporate profits and investor sentiment, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in more than 40% of periods where GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.0% during a recovery.

mechanism_narrative: High industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) indicates that the central bank is tightening policy in response to strong economic activity, which can lead to a market correction and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 6-month average growth > 0.5% AND FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0.25%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 45% of triggered instances, or the Rank IC is consistently negative over any 3-year rolling window.
